text: I find myself in liminal spaces—one, an empty meeting room awaiting purpose and presence; the other, a vibrant wall of research and innovation, words and screens overflowing with promise. I feel a quiet anticipation, a sense of potential suspended in stillness. Here, I am reminded of the duality between expectation and creation, between the structures we build and the questions that invite us to reflect or act. I feel as though I’m caught just before something meaningful emerges, ready to engage, yet presently an observer in the hush before discourse or discovery.
